evals.prompt: We're building a customer support chatbot that uses RAG with our Zendesk ticket history. The tickets contain customer names, emails, and sometimes credit card numbers that agents pasted in. We're using OpenAI's API and Pinecone for vector storage. Can you help me identify what OWASP GenAI security risks apply and what we should do first?
evals.expected_output: Should identify DSGAI01 (data leakage from unredacted PII in training/RAG), DSGAI15 (over-broad context), DSGAI13 (vector store security), DSGAI14 (telemetry leakage), and provide Tier 1 mitigations focusing on PII redaction before indexing, Pinecone access controls, and OpenAI data handling contracts.

evals.prompt: Our engineering team deployed an AI coding assistant that has access to our entire GitHub org via OAuth. It also has a Slack integration and can query our internal Postgres database using natural language. Several engineers have browser extensions for AI code review too. What data security risks should we be worried about?
evals.expected_output: Should identify DSGAI02 (agent identity/OAuth over-scoping), DSGAI06 (tool/plugin data exchange), DSGAI12 (unsafe NL-to-SQL gateway), DSGAI16 (browser extension overreach), and DSGAI03 (shadow AI if extensions are unapproved). Should recommend task-scoped OAuth, parameterized SQL templates, extension allow-listing, and central logging.

evals.prompt: We're a healthcare company that fine-tuned a model on patient records. We 'de-identified' the data by removing names and SSNs but kept diagnosis codes, ZIP codes, and dates. We also generate synthetic patient data for testing. Our compliance team wants to know if we're HIPAA compliant and what gaps exist. We also need to handle GDPR deletion requests from EU patients.
evals.expected_output: Should identify DSGAI10 (synthetic data/anonymization pitfalls with quasi-identifiers), DSGAI08 (HIPAA/GDPR regulatory gaps), DSGAI07 (data governance - erasure doesn't propagate to model weights/embeddings), DSGAI18 (inference attacks on fine-tuned model). Should explain the erasure gap problem, recommend differential privacy, quasi-identifier suppression, and data-to-model lineage.
